title: Maddox v. Board of State Canvassers, 149 P.2d 112 (Mont. 1944)
description: In 1944, both the Republican and Democratic parties of Montana jointly challenged a state law extending the ballot receipt deadline for WWII service members. The Montana Supreme Court struck it down on both state-law and federal grounds, holding that the federal Election Day statute required ballots to be received—not merely cast—by Election Day.
